name: Longinus Spear
description: The Longinus Spear, also known as the Spear of Destiny or Holy Lance, is a legendary artifact steeped in Christian tradition. It is believed to be the spear that pierced the side of Jesus Christ during his crucifixion, as described in the Gospel of John (19:34). The Roman soldier Longinus, who pierced Jesus, is said to have been either blind or suffering from an eye ailment, and his sight was miraculously restored by the blood that flowed from Christ's wound. The spear is thus imbued with immense religious significance, representing both the suffering and sacrifice of Jesus, as well as the potential for redemption and healing. Over the centuries, the Longinus Spear has become a potent symbol of power and destiny. Legend holds that whoever possesses the spear has the power to conquer the world, but also faces inevitable destruction if they lose it. Numerous historical figures, including Roman emperors, Charlemagne, and Adolf Hitler, have been associated with the spear, fueling its mystique and contributing to its enduring allure. The spear is not merely a weapon; it's considered a relic imbued with divine power, a tangible connection to the Passion of Christ, and a reminder of the profound impact of faith and sacrifice. Its existence, whether historical fact or sacred legend, continues to captivate and inspire debate, solidifying its place as one of the most significant artifacts in Christian lore. The spear's power lies not just in its physical form, but in the belief and reverence it inspires. It represents a pivotal moment in Christian history, and embodies the complex interplay of suffering, redemption, and ultimate power. Its story continues to be retold and reinterpreted, ensuring its lasting presence in popular culture and religious discourse.
subtitle: The Spear that pierced Christ.
origin: Used to pierce Jesus Christ during the Crucifixion.
currentLocation: Varying claims; currently in Vienna (Hofburg Palace Treasury).

keyMyths.title: The Piercing of Christ
keyMyths.description: Longinus, a Roman soldier, pierced Jesus' side with the spear to confirm his death. Blood and water flowed from the wound, fulfilling prophecy.

keyMyths.title: The Legend of Power
keyMyths.description: Legend states that whoever possesses the Longinus Spear controls destiny. However, they will suffer a terrible fate if they lose it.

sources.author: Trevor Ravenscroft
sources.description: A popular book exploring the spear's history and association with historical figures.
sources.text: A popular book exploring the spear's history and association with historical figures.
